sections.paragraph:
ἀσύγκριτος, ον (α-priv. + σύγκριτος ‘comparable’; Theophr. et al.; Epicurus [in Diog. L. 10, 83]; Plut., Marcell. 307 [17, 7]; Herm. Wr. 6, 5; GDI 4481, 8 [Laconia]; IGal 110, 8f; BGU 613, 20; PGen 55, 4ff; POxy 1298, 1; Philo; TestLevi 2:9; Jewish ins: RGarrucci, Dissert. Archeol. II 1865, p. 179, no 8; 182 no. 21; Tat. 15, 2) incomparable ISm 13:2 (here w. εὔτεκνος, scarcely a proper name; s. the foll. entry) πρᾶξις ἀ. conduct beyond compare Hm 7:1.—DELG s.v. κρίνω.

priv. priv. = privativum (in grammar: privative, w. negating force)

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

IGal IGal = Exploration archéol. de la Galatie et de la Bithynie—List 3

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PGen PGen = Les Papyrus de Genève—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

foll. foll. = followed, following

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 146
